channel.title: Mulligan Memo
channel.description: Honest golf gear reviews and buying guides — clubs, balls, tech, and accessories.

item.title: How to Organize a Golf Bag: Club Order, Pocket Map, No More Digging
item.description: How to organize a golf bag the right way: exact club order (it flips on a riding cart), a pocket-by-pocket map, divider trade-offs, and a 5-minute purge.

item.title: Golf Simulator Shed Guide: Sizes, Ceiling Height, and What It Really Costs
item.description: How big does a golf simulator shed need to be? Real ceiling, depth, and cost numbers from retailer planning guides — plus climate, power, and permit advice.

item.title: Used Launch Monitors: What to Check and Where the Real Deals Are
item.description: Buying a used launch monitor? Which subscriptions transfer (SkyTrak, Mevo+, Garmin R10), the pre-purchase checklist, and where certified pre-owned deals live.

item.title: Do You Need a Golf Launch Monitor? An Honest Answer
item.description: Do you need a golf launch monitor? An honest look at who actually improves with one, what budget radar really measures, and when lessons beat hardware.

item.title: Best Golf Chipping Nets for Backyard Practice (2026)
item.description: Find the best golf chipping net for backyard practice. Honest chipping net reviews of GoSports, Rukket, SKLZ, Champkey and Spornia, plus drills that transfer.

item.title: Handheld Golf GPS vs Phone App: Is a Dedicated Device Worth It? (2026)
item.description: Handheld golf GPS vs phone app: an honest 2026 breakdown of accuracy, battery drain, subscriptions, and 3-year costs — plus who should buy nothing at all.

item.title: Are Refurbished Golf Balls Worth It? Refurbished vs Recycled, Honestly
item.description: Are refurbished golf balls worth it? Robot testing says rarely — see how refurbished vs recycled golf balls differ, what grades mean, and what to buy instead.

item.title: Best Driver for High Handicappers: The Most Forgiving Heads, Honestly Ranked
item.description: The best driver for high handicappers, honestly ranked: high-MOI 2026 picks, draw-bias options for slicers, loft and shaft advice, and smart used buys.

item.title: Best Golf Chippers for High Handicappers (And Whether You Should Care About the Stigma)
item.description: Find the best golf chipper for your game: six high-handicapper picks, current legality rules on two-way chippers, loft advice, and why the stigma is dead.

item.title: Best Golf Push Cart Under $200: What You Give Up vs the $350 Flagships
item.description: The best golf push cart under $200 in 2026: honest CaddyTek, KVV, Bag Boy and Rovic picks — plus exactly what a $339 Clicgear flagship buys that they don't.

item.title: Best Irons for Beginners and High Handicappers: Wide Soles, No Regrets
item.description: The best irons for beginners, decoded: why wide soles, cavity backs, and offset matter, what set makeup to buy, and honest picks from budget to premium.

item.title: Best Putter for the Yips: What Gear Can (and Can't) Fix
item.description: Searching for the best putter for the yips? Start with an oversized grip, not a $500 putter — the honest, research-backed ladder from grips to broomsticks.

item.title: Cadet vs Regular Golf Gloves: How to Tell Which Fit You Actually Need
item.description: Cadet vs regular golf glove: same letter size, shorter fingers, wider palm. Take the 30-second self-test, read the size chart, and find cadet gloves that fit.

item.title: When Do Golf Clubs Go On Sale? The Best Times to Buy, Month by Month
item.description: When do golf clubs go on sale? Right after new-model launches, at fall closeouts, and on Black Friday. A month-by-month 2026 calendar for buying smarter.

item.title: Best Fairway Woods for High Handicappers (2026): Easier Than a 3-Wood
item.description: The best fairway woods for high handicappers in 2026: why a 5- or 7-wood beats a 3-wood, the loft and shaft basics that help, and forgiving picks.

item.title: Best Golf Balls Under $25 a Dozen (2026): Cheap Balls That Actually Perform
item.description: The best golf balls under $25 a dozen in 2026: which cheap balls actually perform, how to match one to your swing, and the lone budget urethane wildcard.

item.title: Graphite vs Steel Shafts: Which Is Right for Your Irons?
item.description: Graphite vs steel shafts for irons, decided honestly: weight, feel, and cost compared, who each suits, the 2026 heavy-graphite twist, and why a fitting wins.

item.title: Mallet vs Blade Putter: Which Suits Your Stroke? (2026 Buyer's Guide)
item.description: Mallet vs blade putter, decided honestly: it's a stroke-path choice, not a handicap or status one. Take the free finger-balance test before you spend.

item.title: Soft vs Distance Golf Balls (2026): Which Should You Actually Play?
item.description: Soft vs distance golf balls: an honest 2026 guide to what those labels really mean, why soft doesn't add yards, what the robot tests say, and which to play.

item.title: 56 vs 60 Degree Wedge for the Average Golfer: Do You Need Both?
item.description: 56 vs 60 degree wedge for the average golfer: why most players need a 56 and a gap wedge — not a 60 — plus exact beginner wedge setups by pitching-wedge loft.

item.title: Best Golf Club Set Under $500 for Beginners (2026 Guide)
item.description: Find the best golf club set under $500 for beginners in 2026: how to choose for forgiveness, shaft, and fit, plus honest, no-hype picks from trusted brands.

item.title: Best Driver for Seniors and Slow Swing Speeds (2026)
item.description: The best driver for seniors in 2026: how loft, weight, and flex help slower swings, plus honest picks for forgiveness, value, and slice help, and fitting tips.

item.title: Best Golf Balls for Beginners (2026): Soft, Straight & Affordable
item.description: The best golf balls for beginners in 2026: soft, straight, durable, and cheap picks that suit slower swings, plus what compression means and when to upgrade.

item.title: Best Golf Balls for Distance (2026): Maximum Carry and Roll
item.description: The best golf balls for distance in 2026: how low-spin, firm-cover construction adds carry and roll, who really gains yards, and our honest 2026 picks.

item.title: Best Golf Balls for Slow Swing Speeds (2026 Buyer's Guide)
item.description: The best golf balls for slow swing speeds in 2026: low-compression picks for seniors and sub-90-mph swings, what compression really does, and when to pay more.

item.title: Best Golf Glove for Sweaty Hands (Grip That Lasts)
item.description: The best golf glove for sweaty hands is usually a rain or breathable synthetic glove that grips when wet, not leather. Our guide covers picks, rotation, care.

item.title: Best Golf GPS Watch Under $200 (2026 Buyer's Guide)
item.description: The best golf GPS watch under $200 in 2026: honest picks for accuracy, battery, and subscription-free yardages. Garmin S12, Bushnell iON Elite and TecTecTec.

item.title: Best Golf Hybrids for High Handicappers (Most Forgiving Picks)
item.description: The best golf hybrids for high handicappers: the most forgiving, easiest-to-hit models, how to pick the right loft, which irons to replace, and budget picks.

item.title: Best Golf Irons for Seniors (2026): Light, Forgiving, Easy to Launch
item.description: The best golf irons for seniors in 2026: light, forgiving, easy-to-launch sets. Graphite vs steel, senior flex, top picks, and honest, no-hype buying advice.

item.title: Best Golf Launch Monitor Under $500: Affordable 2026 Picks
item.description: The cheapest golf launch monitors worth buying in 2026: honest affordable picks under $500, subscription traps, and what budget units measure vs estimate.

item.title: Best Golf Putters Under $100 (2026): Mallets & Blades That Roll Pure
item.description: The best golf putters under $100 in 2026: honest mallet and blade picks, the new-vs-used truth on famous models, and the free fitting test that matters most.

item.title: Best Golf Rangefinder for Shaky Hands & Hand Tremors (Image-Stabilized Picks)
item.description: The best golf rangefinder for shaky hands needs real image stabilization, not just a buzz. OIS vs vibration lock-on explained, plus our top stabilized picks.

item.title: Best Golf Rangefinder Under $100: Are Cheap Ones Accurate?
item.description: The best golf rangefinder under $100 in 2026: are cheap lasers as accurate as a Bushnell? Honest picks for slope, beginners, and tournament-legal play.

item.title: Golf Rangefinder vs GPS: Which Should You Buy?
item.description: Golf rangefinder vs GPS, explained without the hype: how laser and GPS distance compare on accuracy, speed, slope rules, and price — plus which one fits you.

item.title: Best Golf Shoes for Wide Feet (2026): Comfort Without the Pinch
item.description: The best golf shoes for wide feet in 2026: buy true length in a wider width (not a size up), the brands with real wide SKUs, plus honest reputation-based picks.

item.title: Best Golf Training Aids That Actually Work (2026)
item.description: The best golf training aids that actually work in 2026 — alignment, tempo, contact, grip, and putting — plus this year's gimmick watch on what to skip.

item.title: Best Golf Wedges Under $100 for 2026 (Spin Without the Splurge)
item.description: The best golf wedges under $100 for 2026: forgiving picks, the loft and bounce that fit your swing, and the truth about whether cheap wedges really spin.

item.title: Best Left Handed Golf Club Sets (2026 Buyer's Guide)
item.description: The best left handed golf club sets for 2026: honest picks for beginners, seniors, women, and value buyers, plus how to find lefty gear and confirm stock first.

item.title: Best Putting Mat for Home: Top Picks for 2026
item.description: The best putting mat for home in 2026, ranked by true roll, speed, and value — from PrimePutt to budget picks — plus honest limits, sizing, and setup tips.

item.title: Best Used Irons for a Mid Handicapper on a Budget (2026)
item.description: The best used irons for a mid handicapper on a budget: game-improvement vs players-distance, models to hunt under $400, where to buy, and what to check.

item.title: Best Womens Complete Golf Set for Beginners (2026 Picks)
item.description: The best womens complete golf set for beginners in 2026: how to choose for forgiveness, flex and the right fit (tall or petite), plus honest, no-hype picks.

item.title: Callaway Strata Review (2026): Is the Complete Set Worth It for Beginners?
item.description: An honest Callaway Strata review for 2026: the missing sand wedge, the weak putter, 12-piece vs 14-piece, and whether this beginner set is worth the money.

item.title: Callaway Strata vs Wilson Profile SGI: Which Beginner Set Wins?
item.description: Callaway Strata vs Wilson Profile SGI compared: what's in each box, the piece-count myth, Wilson's tall/petite/senior fits, wedges, putters, and a verdict.

item.title: Cleveland Launcher XL2 Driver Review: Best Budget Driver for Slicers & Slow Swings?
item.description: Our Cleveland Launcher XL2 driver review for slicers and slow swings: who needs the Draw model, honest sound caveats, the right loft and shaft, plus rivals.

item.title: Complete Golf Set vs Buying Clubs Individually: Which Is Better for Beginners?
item.description: Complete golf set vs buying clubs individually: the real cost gap, what to buy used (and the one club to always buy new), and which path is best for beginners.

item.title: Driver vs 3 Wood Off the Tee for High Handicappers: Which to Hit?
item.description: Driver vs 3 wood off the tee for high handicappers: the data says driver. The distance gap, the accuracy myth, when to lay back, plus forgiving drivers.

item.title: The Best Golf Ball for Your Game: A 2026 Buyer's Guide
item.description: How to choose the right golf ball for your swing speed and handicap in 2026 — compression, cover, and spin explained, plus our category-by-category picks.

item.title: Golf Grip Size Chart: How to Choose the Right Grip Size
item.description: A clear golf grip size chart plus how to measure your hand, the standard vs midsize vs jumbo specs, a glove cross-check, and how grip size affects ball flight.

item.title: Best Golf Grips for Arthritic Hands: A Senior Golfer's Buying Guide (2026)
item.description: The best golf grips for arthritic hands let you hold the club with less squeezing and absorb more shock. Our guide covers sizing, soft vs. cord, and 5 picks.

item.title: Golf Rangefinder Slope vs Non-Slope: Which Should You Buy?
item.description: Golf rangefinder slope vs non slope, explained simply: what slope does, whether it's worth it, how to stay tournament-legal, and 6 real switchable picks.

item.title: How Many Golf Clubs Does a Beginner Need? Minimalist Starter Bag
item.description: How many golf clubs does a beginner need? The cap is 14, but there's no minimum. Here's the ideal 7-10 club starter setup, what to skip, and what to buy.

item.title: How to Clean Golf Clubs (Irons, Woods & Grips) the Right Way
item.description: How to clean golf clubs the right way: a beginner-proof guide to washing irons, wedges, woods, drivers and grips at home, plus rust fixes and frequency.

item.title: How to Regrip Golf Clubs at Home (Step-by-Step + What You Need)
item.description: How to regrip golf clubs at home: a step-by-step guide to the tools you need, cutting off old grips, taping, solvent, alignment, drying time, and DIY cost.

item.title: Hybrid vs Long Iron: Which Should You Carry?
item.description: Hybrid vs long iron, settled honestly: the physics, the loft-replacement map, real 2026 Shot Scope data, when a long iron still wins, and which hybrids to buy.

item.title: Junior Golf Club Size Chart by Height (Age & Height Buying Guide)
item.description: A junior golf club size chart by height, plus how to measure your kid at home, a quick age cross-check, and which US Kids, Callaway XJ or Cobra set fits.

item.title: Kirkland KS1 Putter vs Scotty Cameron: Is the Costco Putter Actually Worth It?
item.description: Kirkland KS1 putter vs Scotty Cameron: an honest review of feel, build, and price. The Costco putter gives most of a milled blade for about a third the cost.

item.title: 3 Wheel vs 4 Wheel Golf Push Cart: Which Should You Buy?
item.description: 3 wheel vs 4 wheel golf push cart, decided. Compare stability, turning, fold size and terrain, plus honest, reputation-based picks for every walking golfer.

item.title: Senior Flex vs Regular Flex Shaft: Which Should You Play?
item.description: Senior flex vs regular flex shaft, explained honestly: real swing-speed ranges, the 80-90 mph overlap, too-stiff vs too-soft signs, and how to test it.

item.title: Spikeless vs Spiked Golf Shoes: Which Should You Buy?
item.description: Spikeless vs spiked golf shoes, decided by conditions and play style: traction, comfort, walkability, durability, and honest reputation-based picks for both.

item.title: Cart Bag vs Stand Bag: Which Golf Bag Should You Buy?
item.description: Cart bag vs stand bag, settled by how you actually play. Weight, dividers, pockets, push-cart fit, and our reputation-based picks for walkers and riders.

item.title: What Driver Loft Should I Use? A Simple Guide by Swing Speed
item.description: What driver loft should I use? A plain guide to choosing driver loft by swing speed and attack angle — 9 vs 10.5 vs 12 degree, slice myths, and fitting tips.

item.title: Winn Dri-Tac vs Golf Pride CP2 Wrap: Which Soft Grip Wins?
item.description: Winn Dri-Tac vs Golf Pride CP2 Wrap: the Winn is softer and best in the wet; the CP2 Wrap lasts longer and adds stability. Our honest pick for sore hands.
